rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Token Transfer in liquidate() Function
rejected_candidates.reason: `liquidate()` ends by pulling the required MIM shares from `msg.sender` via BentoBox. If the liquidator / swapper has not actually produced those shares, the transfer reverts and the earlier collateral transfer is rolled back with the transaction.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked Repayment in repayForAll() Function
rejected_candidates.reason: `repayForAll()` only reduces `totalBorrow.elastic` after either depositing the contract's actual MIM balance (`skim=true`) or transferring BentoBox shares from `msg.sender` (`skim=false`). Missing funds cause the BentoBox operation to revert.

rejected_candidates.title: No Access Control on reduceSupply() Allows Theft
Oracle Rate Manipulation for Liquidations
rejected_candidates.reason: The report assumes a flash-loan-manipulable oracle, but only the generic oracle interface is present here. Without a vulnerable oracle implementation, this is unsupported speculation.

rejected_candidates.title: Unrestricted Swapper in Liquidation
rejected_candidates.reason: An arbitrary swapper is a caller-chosen execution path, but the protocol does not accept underpayment: the transaction reverts unless the liquidator ultimately transfers the full required MIM shares back to the Cauldron.

rejected_candidates.title: No Reentrancy Guards on Critical Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Too generic and unsupported. The report does not identify a concrete reentrant callback path that bypasses BentoBox accounting or the post-action solvency checks.

rejected_candidates.title: setFeeTo Has No Timelock or Multi-Sig
rejected_candidates.reason: This is governance hardening advice, not a protocol vulnerability in the contract logic.

rejected_candidates.title: Liquidate Allows Zero Collateral Extraction
rejected_candidates.reason: With `maxBorrowPart = 0`, `borrowPart`, `borrowAmount`, and `collateralShare` all stay zero. There is no path to extract collateral for free.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited BentoBox Token Approval
rejected_candidates.reason: Approving the core BentoBox dependency is an explicit trust assumption of the design, not a distinct vulnerability in this Cauldron implementation.

rejected_candidates.title: Interest Rate Can Be Set to Zero
rejected_candidates.reason: Allowing the owner to choose a low rate is a governance/configuration choice. The reportable bug is the retroactive repricing caused by changing the rate without accruing first.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Validation for Strategy Release in Cook
rejected_candidates.reason: The absence of extra checks for an active strategy or strategy funds is not itself harmful. The only plausible issue here is the permissionless rebalance exposure captured separately with lower confidence.
